paragraphs: Can't be unseen. Can't be unthought. [#pause]And, the gooey, yeah. Don't touch that. [#pause]Don't even think about touching that.

sections.heading:
14 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
"THE LEAF" MAIREE DELTA

paragraphs.text: "[screaming]FUUUCK!" Kirsten Strand's screams reached the camp first.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: The spore storm was over, and the jungle appeared peaceful again. They were a man short. A boy. The space where Nasr should have been was a gaping wound in their formation.
Once inside the air shield, the squad scattered. Jordan Axel gave Kirsten Strand a weary look of shared grief before turning away.

Kirsten didn't stop at the washing station. [#muddy-boots]She marched straight through camp, her heavy boots muddying the deck plates. She entered her post and [#doorslam]slammed the door, and for a moment, it was quiet. Then [#crash]a crash, [#roar-female2]followed by another roar from within.
A pulse later, Sarah followed her in. The station was already a mess. A supply crate lay thrown against the far wall.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Kirsten, please," Sarah said softly. "[soft]This is not your fault."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[curt]Get out," Kirsten said, her back still turned away. Her broad shoulders shuddered. She barely contained her anger, and it was threatening to spill over.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[firm]I am the one responsible."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[bitter]Responsible?" Kirsten's eyes were full of grief and anger. "[bitter]You think this is about duty? I lost a kid. A kid! He was barely twenty."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[unflinching]I know," Sarah replied, unflinching. "[unflinching]I was there."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[roaring]Tell that to his family! Tell them how you were there. Tell them what you did to stop me!" Kirsten roared.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[steady]And how would *you* have it?" Sarah asked, chin up.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[venomous]How dare you!" Kirsten's roar sank to a venomous quiet. "[venomous]I'd have the boy back." She stepped closer, voice a low, trembling growl. "[trembling]Rule one: My job is to bring my people home. All of them. Yours is to learn from this FUCKING HELLHOLE so it doesn't swallow another recruit!"
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: The anger left Kirsten's eyes. It had burned her out. Kirsten slumped onto the overturned crate. Sarah moved beside her, and, after a pause, put an arm around Kirsten's armored shoulders. She seemed much smaller now. She rested her head against Sarah's, their tears touching, sealing a peace words couldn't have brokered.

paragraphs.text: Sarah took Kirsten's trembling hand and pressed it flat against her own chest. "[soft]Do you feel that?" Sarah said softly. "[soft]My heart is racing.[#pause]I'm scared. [#pause2]I need you."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Alani Jimenez and Veteran Jordan Axel had stayed together, cleaning and sorting their equipment in an ashen silence. A figure approached them. Marco Thieman, the leader of the other rangers. He smelled of citrus from the insect-repelling ointment. His squad was gearing up behind him, ready to head out.

paragraphs.text: "[grim]Axel. Jimenez," Thieman greeted them with a grim nod as he approached. "[grim]Nasr." He shook his head, a gesture of shared, weary loss. "[weary]It's a bitch of a world."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs.text: "[level]Are you heading out, sir?" Axel asked.
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

paragraphs.text: Thieman smiled, but there was no joy in it. "[joyless]Indeed. We're going underground. Found another vurnshaft that might lead all the way down into a node."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He [#tap]tapped his wristpad.

paragraphs.text: "[focused]We could map a nexus."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: Thieman was a wiry man who appeared permanently tanned.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]So late?" Alani asked quietly, looking up from his rifle. "[quiet]And after what happened to Nasr?"
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: Thieman gazed at the darkening sky and the trail ahead.

paragraphs.text: "[ominous]Before long, she will eat us all."
paragraphs.speaker: marco-thieman

paragraphs: He gave them a final, curt nod before his team headed out and got swallowed by the gloom of the jungle.

paragraphs.text: Axel finished reassembling his rifle. "[quiet]He's right," he said quietly. "[quiet]Before long."
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

sections.heading: DAWN
paragraphs: Sarah pushed herself upright, straightened her jacket, and, with stiff shoulders and slow steps, made her way into the camp. The morning light crept sluggishly between the tents. The humid air was filled with the pungent smell of scorched vines. From above, the Leaf was a scar in the forest that kept trying to close over it. Each day, teams with plasma cutters fought back the creeping vines.
The water purifiers at the river delta were always in need of repairs, and the crew was already stretched too thin. The Directorate was holding back supplies from orbit.
She stepped into the command tent as [#alert-pong]a priority alert chimed from the main comms console. The message came from a water outpost by the river.

paragraphs.text: "[#static2][urgent]This is DROP Seven; we have an injury."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Copy, DROP Seven, this is the Leaf," Sarah responded. "[focused]Sitrep, please."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[#static2][strained]Rojas fell. Bad. Leg's broken. We need a medevac now, or a doctor here. We're running on empty."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: An internal tent door zipped open. Kirsten stepped through, her armor now spotless.

paragraphs.text: "[#static2][strained]There's more," the engineer on the comm said. "[strained]A pack of clackers has moved into the shallows."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Sarah knew these creatures: two meters long, semi-aquatic, with strong paddle-shaped limbs and a beak that could crush giant shellfish.

paragraphs.text: "[#static2][worried]And now there are dartswarms too."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: They were hand-sized, dragonfly-like bugs with shiny, saw-edged wings. A swarm could dive at someone and strip flesh in seconds. Survivors often got infections from their venomous bites and cuts.

paragraphs.text: "[#static2][desperate]We need help. We're spread too thin. Without Rojas…"
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Kirsten's eyes went to the pulsing red icon on the mission board that showed the river outpost.

paragraphs.text: "[resolved]I'm ready," she said with resolve.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[decisive]We'll hop over," Sarah decided.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Kirsten gaze went from the comms console to the landing pad outside, where the utility transport waited. The Hopper was a fast but notoriously unstable vehicle. She looked Sarah directly in the eye, raising an eyebrow. Sarah pointed at the large, sealed medical crates by the entrance. Kirsten gave a quick nod.

paragraphs.text: Sarah paused. "[curious]You ever flown in one of those before?"
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: Sarah's mind flicked to the report of one that had recently crashed due to malfunctioning stabilizers.
"[curt]No."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: Sarah smirked. "[wry]Just strap in tight."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: The Hopper was a flying flatbed truck. It was a square, armored platform, five meters on each side, powered by gravitic thrusters. There was no sleek hull or cockpit, only a compact control console, two fold-out seats with harnesses, magnetic clamps, and a cargo net. It was designed for emergency jumps from one place to another.

Once the medical crates were secured, Kirsten strapped herself in and locked her magboots to the deck. Sarah took the seat beside her and started up the controls. [#alarm-groan]The Hopper's power cells kicked in with a low [#shuddering-plates]groan, making the deck plates shake. The [#thruster]gravitic thrusters' coils glowed blue-white. [#claxon]A klaxon sounded twice, warning of an imminent grav-lift departure. The air around the platform shimmered.

paragraphs.text: "[yelling]Here we go!" Sarah yelled over the noise, gripping the controls.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Wait!"
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: Someone was sprinting across the camp toward them. It was Jimenez, fumbling to clip his field pack onto his armor.

paragraphs.text: "[bellowing]Jimenez, get back!" Kirsten bellowed.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[defiant]Negative!" he shouted, not slowing down.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: [#alarm-groan]The klaxon blared a final warning.

paragraphs.text: "[panicked]Too late!" Sarah yelled. "[panicked]I can't abort!"
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: Jimenez ignored her and kept running. He dashed and, at the last second, vaulted over the side into the Hopper as it launched as if fired by a slingshot. It pressed Kirsten deep into her seat, while Jimenez was sucked onto the deck. For five terrifying seconds, they rocketed like a cannonball, curving above the jungle canopy.

Fighting the Gs, Jimenez grabbed a handhold and [#magboot]locked his magboots as they reached the height of their jump. The upward thrust cut out. For a long, stomach-turning moment, Kirsten felt weightless. Jimenez gasped and steadied himself.

paragraphs.text: "[growling]You," Kirsten growled from her harness, "[growling]are on my shit-list for the next click."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[gasping]Yes, ma'am," Jimenez gasped, a triumphant, adrenaline-fueled grin on his face.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs.text: "[sharp]'Ma'am'?! That's two shit-clicks!"
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Then they fell. The main thruster fell silent, but the smaller [#thruster-burst]maneuvering thrusters fired in rough, [#thruster-burst]rattling bursts, keeping the platform mostly steady. They began a nerve-wracking glide toward the river.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Brace!" Sarah yelled.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: The landing was no more graceful than the launch. At the last moment, [#thruster-burst]a strong reverse thrust kicked in to slow them down. The Hopper [#rattling]shuddered again as they hit the landing pad's anti-grav field. They [#hard-landing]slammed onto a plateau by the soggy riverbank with a bone-jarring thump. The thrusters wound down to silence. For a moment, [#insects]Kirsten couldn't tell the frantic chirping of insects from the ringing in her ears.

paragraphs.text: Kirsten unclipped her harness and walked over to Jimenez, who was groaning on the floor. "[growling]Where's that smile now, huh?!" she growled, leaning in and pulling him up. "[ordering]Now grab that crate and make yourself useful."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: Jimenez got to his feet, holding his head. "[dazed]Whoa," he muttered, swaying as dizziness hit him.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs: He tried to step and winced. Landing without support had cost him more than his dignity.

paragraphs.text: "[grunting]Serves you right," Kirsten grunted. "[grunting]Walk it off."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Sarah grabbed her medkit and followed Kirsten, who had already started toward the outpost. They were met halfway by a smudgy, breathless, and wild-eyed engineer.

paragraphs.text: "[breathless]Thank you," the man gasped out. "[breathless]Wittler. Leon. Hydrology Tech Lead."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Sarah picked up her pace and hurried past Wittler into the main hall, Kirsten not far behind. Rojas lay on a cot in the corner, his face pale. Sarah rushed to his side and cut away the makeshift splint around his leg. Her face grew serious when she saw the exposed bone from the compound fracture.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]I can stabilize you," she told him, steady and calm. "[steady]But you won't make a hop. Not like this. You really do need a medevac."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: She started filling out a request on her slate. An instant later, her slate chimed.
< [#chime]FOX | MEDEVAC UNAVAILABLE />

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]What's your first name, Rojas?" she asked.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[stuttering]Ka-Kenji," he stuttered, unclenching his jaw.
paragraphs.speaker: kenji-rojas

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Lie still, Kakenji."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[strained]Nnnggg. Just. Kenji," he corrected.
paragraphs.speaker: kenji-rojas

paragraphs.text: "[soothing]Be still, okay?" she said as she started cleaning the wound. "[focused]It's a rough break. The fibula is damaged, but the tibia looks fine."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[pleading]J-Just patch me up. I'll risk the hop."
paragraphs.speaker: kenji-rojas

paragraphs.text: Sarah gave him a stern look. "[stern]You're not going anywhere, bud. Not like this."
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Clackers at the pump housing! They're tearing it apart!" Wittler shouted.
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Kirsten ran back out. [#river-short]Cracking and splintering [#wood-groan-river]sounded from the river, then the [#metal-screech]screech of metal tearing. Shadows shifted in the water. Then, behind her, [#hopper-thrust]the Hopper roared again. It was timed to return to its base pad at the Leaf. On its own, it veered drunkenly, one side dipping precariously as a thruster failed to fire in sync. It swatted a large bird from the air, spraying its colorful feathers. The vehicle wobbled before the thrusters wrenched it back onto its ballistic trajectory toward the Leaf.

paragraphs.text: Wittler put his hand on Kirsten's shoulder. "[grim]It's the river. It's rising."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs: Kirsten eyed the lanky man. His greasy, sandy-blond hair hung in his eyes. Around his right eye, his cheekbone was swollen and purple. His tanned skin was dirty, covered with cuts and scratches.

paragraphs.text: "[strained]The clackers…" He pointed with a shaking finger at the main pump, where the animals were chewing on a support strut. "[planning]We could disrupt them with the pump's sonic emitters. Drive them off for a while."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs.text: "[focused]How?" Kirsten stared at the huge creatures.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[explaining]With your help. The sonic emitters, for mapping the riverbed, can be made to send focused pulses into the riverbed. I can aim them at the clackers at the base of the pylon over there." Wittler glanced at the dartswarm between them and the pump. "[wary]But the swarm's in the way."
paragraphs.speaker: leon-wittler

paragraphs.text: Kirsten turned to Jimenez. "[commanding]Let's cover him. Use a wide pattern. Clear a path. You left."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[disciplined]On it, First Guardian." Jimenez winced and grabbed his ankle, then got into position.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs.text: "[roaring]Fire!" Strand roared.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: [#insects2]The insects' high-pitched whine turned into furious shrieking.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Run!"
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Wittler ran in a desperate sprint across the muddy ground to the pump's control panel. Wittler broke from cover.
Kirsten tracked him through the sights as he sprinted across the mud, boots [#mud-squelch]slipping, arms pumping too wide. The swarm folded toward him.
He skidded into the pump's control panel with enough force to rattle it.

paragraphs.text: "[muttered]Come on," she muttered.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He tore the access plate free. It [#wet-slap]dropped into the mud with a wet slap.
Wittler swung the emitter toward the river.
[#sonic-emitter]The water puckered and rippled, racing toward the pylon where the clackers clustered.

The swarm adjusted to the covering fire, and a part of it dove at him. The dragonflies slashed his face, arms, and back. Still, he didn't stop. He hit the controls again, sending [#sonic-emitter]a second burst. The clackers flinched; their bodies shuddered. They left the pylon and scuttled back through the murky water. Wittler staggered toward the shelter, holding his bleeding arm.

paragraphs.text: The air smelled of burnt chitin. "[shouting]They're ignoring the pulse fire!" Jimenez shouted.
paragraphs.speaker: alani-jimenez

paragraphs.text: Kirsten didn't waste another energy cell. "[barked]Flash-bang!" She reached for a canister on her belt.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Jimenez did the same, twisted the cap, and threw the grenade into the muddy ground directly between them and the retreating Wittler. [#flash-bang]It blew the darts apart. Wittler tripped but got up, half-blinded, and continued.
Kirsten grabbed him around his waist, supporting him into the hall. Jimenez followed, [#doorslam]slamming the door shut. [#dartswarm-door]The swarm rained on it like a hailstorm.

Kirsten eased Wittler down. He lowered his hands from where they had been clamped to his ears, then looked at them as if checking for blood. Sarah had stabilized Kenji and was now moving over to Wittler.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Lie still," she said, pressing a diagnostic scanner to a cut.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: The device [#scanner]whined as it analyzed the darts' venom proteins. Analysis complete, she leaned into a medical crate and filled a syringe with a yellow antidote.
Leon drew back at the sight of the needle. His shoulders tensed. Fingers curled protectively around his arm. He looked every bit the reluctant patient.

paragraphs.text: "[warning]This is going to burn," Sarah warned, then injected.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs: He grunted. For a moment, he stared at the wall, breath shallow, nostrils flared.

paragraphs.text: "[dry]You'll live," Sarah said, now checking the cuts.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: After a brief silence, Kenji Rojas spoke up. "[bitter]We're never going to meet the water quota under these conditions. We're just a small station, for fuck's sake! There's no way we should be responsible for twenty percent of potable water replenishment."
paragraphs.speaker: kenji-rojas

paragraphs.text: Kirsten checked her wristband. "[quiet]Steady signal through O3; it can momentarily route us through to FOX," she suggested quietly.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[decisive]Let's try," Sarah replied.
paragraphs.speaker: sarah-farley

paragraphs.text: Kirsten opened a direct call to FreyOps Central. "[formal]I'm at DROP Seven. We can't hold this position. We're besieged by aggressive wildlife, and the river is rising. This station is vital for our water supply."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: Instead of a liaison, she heard Centurion Jax.

paragraphs.text: "[cold]First Guardian. It is your duty to secure it with the detail you've been assigned. We are preparing to deploy an entire squadron in five clicks. Hold out till then."
paragraphs.speaker: centurion-jax

paragraphs: Kirsten took in the room. Sarah wiped blood from her gloves. Rojas's outburst had taken it out of him, and he now lay pale and immobile on the cot. Wittler was hunched and shivering. Jimenez limped to a water canister, only to realize it was empty.

paragraphs.text: "[strained]Five clicks is going to be a stretch," Kirsten muttered to her slate.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[cold]Then stretch it," Jax replied without compassion and closed the call.
paragraphs.speaker: centurion-jax

paragraphs: Kirsten pressed her lips together, forcing down a scream that rose in her throat. Sarah looked at her with tired understanding. Wittler shuddered, his arm snapping tight against his side. His fingers clawed at nothing. Jimenez wiped sweat from his brow, rifle resting across his knees.

paragraphs.text: Kirsten turned to the crew. "[resolved]We'll hold."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: As she spoke, [#thruster2]the Hopper's whine reverberated outside. Kirsten gazed out the window to see its thrusters scattering the swarm. [#hard-landing2]It landed with a thump on the pad. A guardian stepped out. Kirsten threw the door open, gesturing for him to hurry inside.

paragraphs.text: "[bellowing]Heard you were having a party without me," Axel bellowed over the noise.
paragraphs.speaker: jordan-axel

sections.heading:
15 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
TIC, FNS DÁINN
paragraphs: Like a resisting pawn forced back into formation against its will, the Dáinn returned course toward Mairee. Thump after thump, Orbital Command sent messages urging them to move faster. Captain Ben Westhoff ignored them. He leaned forward, looking at a photo pinned to the wall. It showed Fleet Commander Astrid Vilde smiling alongside all the fleet's captains. Ben was standing next to Astrid, their arms around each other.
The Tactical Information Center now held four people: Ben himself, XO Ionie Jia, Chief Dominic Beltrán, and Quartermaster Steven Gail.

paragraphs.text: Ben turned from the photo and sat down. "[gravelly]Let's look at the board," he said with a gravelly drawl.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: Jia activated the holotable. "[informative]On Mairee, Corbin has been busy setting up his Directorate and something called 'the Grid.' He clearly wants total control over everyone and everything, and it's all happening openly. People at the Homestead, the Plateau, and even those in orbit are affected. It won't be long before he expands to the Delta. He uses the Grid to hand out rations, limit movement, and curate communications. It even drugs people to keep them docile. He's recruiting young officers to expand the guard."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: "[contrarian]Maybe," Gail cut in, clear and sharp, "[contrarian]we should think about whether Corbin's actions, even if unpleasant, might be right."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: Gail was pale and thinner. Ben noticed he'd lost a lot of weight in transit. His uniform lay smooth, the sharp lines of his shoulders and collarbones showing through the fabric. The grooming of his deliberate beard, where his mustache charismatically extended out and up, had not been neglected during the journey.

paragraphs.text: "[smirking]Let me play devil's advocate," Gail went on, fidgeting with his cuff, a slight smirk on his face. "[smirking]Our supply line is gone. Our lifeline. People will panic, and when they do, they'll fight. Hysteria. We don't have the supplies or the time to waste on that. When the Fold closed, everything changed. Corbin understood the consequences right away."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Jia narrowed her eyes. "[pointed]Yes, but his first move was hostile."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: Gail looked up, thinking. "[conceding]Fair point," he said, waving his hand. "[contrarian]But waiting might still have been worse."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Ben spoke. "[firm]That's right. If we give up our humanity, we're already extinct."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: Jia nodded. "[firm]The Grid violates the charter. We need to shut it down before it traps us."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: Ben pressed his lips. Not yet, he told himself. I'll inform her soon.
"[probing]And how do you suggest we do that?" Gail asked, leaning forward a little.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: Beltrán was slumped back. When he tried to sit upright, the motion stalled halfway.

paragraphs.text: "[wry]If we mess with the web, the spider will know right away."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: Jia pressed her hand on the table. "[resolute]We rally the Bygul and Trjegul. With the fleet, we blockade Mairee."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: Gail chuckled. "[mocking]Blockade who? The people you want to save? That's Corbin's playbook."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[firm]He's right," Ben said, breaking through the argument. "[grave]Astrid Vilde isn't his only hostage. We can't win by fighting him head-on."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: The room went quiet.

paragraphs.text: "[measured]But he's following the Emergency Suppression Protocol," Westhoff went on, looking at each person in turn. "[measured]That protocol says a council, a new governing body, must be formed once the threat is suppressed. It doesn't allow for the director to remain in charge."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[musing]He's a logistician," Gail mused, "[musing]not a politician."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[firm]It's a start," Ben said. "[firm]It establishes legitimacy."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[low]He'll use levers you won't, Captain. You know what I mean." Gail's voice dropped.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Beltrán broke the silence. "[measured]We will, of course, surrender the Dáinn. And you, Ben, will be relieved of your command. That is what Corbin won't question. It's exactly what he'll have in mind."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Jia was about to protest. But Ben held up a hand to stop her.

paragraphs.text: Beltrán continued, "[scheming]While the spider admires his new fly, he won't be ready for the web we've woven."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Ben shifted in his chair, uneasy. His physical position mirrored his position of authority—neither was working for him anymore.

paragraphs.text: "[planning]Keep it clean and believable. We'll stage a minor protest, but Corbin gets the win. Jia, draft a message to my senior staff. Say we'll comply with Directorate protocols, but make it sound as if I had no choice." Ben rubbed his wrist, frustrated.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: The XO nodded.

paragraphs.text: Ben turned to the Quartermaster. "[probing]Steven. Who are our friends on the Plateau?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: Gail's faint smirk returned. "[measured]I can identify personnel with whom we can work. But, Captain, I don't think they can move goods unseen or undetected. Whatever we ship down will be monitored. Corbin will make sure to track every last gram according to the manifest."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[firm]That's right. Everything according to the manifest. But that's not all I plan to send down there," Ben said.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]People, too?" Jia asked quietly.
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: "[measured]Maybe," Westhoff said. "[measured]But first, we need someone inside the Directorate. We have to find out what Corbin plans for me. I'm not going to end up in a biostasis pod next to our Fleet Commander."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: Beltrán's voice was low. "[low]Muro. All comms pass through 'The Ears.' I'm already in touch."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: Jia jumped in. "[wary]Maybe they're on to us. Maybe that's why Corbin felt confident using Vilde as leverage."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: "[concerned]Good point," Gail said. "[probing]Can we make sure Muro's not compromised?"
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: Beltrán surprised everyone by pushing himself out of his chair. He dragged his feet across the deck as if he were wearing magnetic boots set far too heavy. He reached the table and leaned on it to keep himself from spinning.

paragraphs.text: "[explaining]The orbital insertion procedure is full of routine checks and system handshakes. It also contains a lot of legacy code. We'll add more nonsense, bad jokes, and puns. At first, it'll seem like we're fooling around. If they get suspicious, they'll try to decode it and find nothing. They'll think I'm being a Nulgee. But when we're closer, I'll whisper a key to Muro. With it, we can start making some poetry even Vogons will appreciate."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: Ben nodded. "[focused]Alright. That gives us a potential line to Directorate intel." He held up a second finger. "[focused]Next, the Grid."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[reflective]The Grid…" Gail frowned. "[reflective]The COG was Kaspar Savannen's work, rest in peace. His son's a smart kid, always tinkering."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[probing]You think this kid can help?" Ben leaned forward.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[noncommittal]Maybe." Gail shrugged. "[measured]If we can find him. He'd be a civ at the Homestead. We were also running a version of the COG on this ship."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Ben smiled and raised a third finger, and his face turned grim. "[grim]And lastly, there's Vilde."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: The name hung in the air. "[stating]She's in the Homestead stasis bay," Jia stated. "[stating]Undoubtedly guarded."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs: Ben shifted from face to face. No one had an answer. Even Beltrán squared his shoulders.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]We'll figure it out," Ben said quietly. "[quiet]Let's work with what we have for now."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff
